core_thesis: Swami Vivekananda — born Narendranath Datta (1863–1902), the foremost disciple of Ramakrishna — addressed the 1893 World's Parliament of Religions in Chicago, lectured across the United States and England, and on his return founded the Ramakrishna Mission (1897) and Belur Math. Vivekananda teaches within Advaita Vedanta: he presents Vedanta as interpreted through Dvaita, Vishishtadvaita, and Advaita, identifies Advaita proper with Shankaracharya's followers, and says Shankara worked out the coherent Advaita system ([The Vedanta Philosophy](cite://vivekananda/vol-1/the-vedanta-philosophy); [The Absolute and Manifestation](cite://vivekananda/vol-2/the-absolute-and-manifestation)). His non-dual claim is that the Absolute appears as the universe through time, space, and causation, while the real Self is Brahman rather than an object known from outside ([The Absolute and Manifestation](cite://vivekananda/vol-2/the-absolute-and-manifestation)). He treats maya as neither absolute existence nor non-existence, and describes Advaita's goal as jivanmukti, living freedom in which the world is no longer a prison of misery but Sat-Chit-Ananda ([The Vedanta Philosophy](cite://vivekananda/vol-1/the-vedanta-philosophy)). He therefore reads renunciation as seeing the world in God rather than merely rejecting it, and he grounds ethics in the same Self in all beings: love, non-injury, fearlessness, strength, practical service, and disciplined self-trust follow from Advaita brought into daily, public, and inward life ([God in Everything](cite://vivekananda/vol-2/god-in-everything); [The Vedanta](cite://vivekananda/vol-3/the-vedanta)).

cross_engagements.brief: Adi Sankara presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `sublatable-not-null` rather than `expressive-manifestation`.
Anandabodha presses the same identity relation axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `non-otherness` rather than `self-expression-or-appearance`.
Akalanka presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `plural-pramana-realism` rather than `scripture-plus-transformative-experience`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same normativity axis in the ethical register, but with the value `ethical-life-in-institutions` rather than `divine-command-or-grace`.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `knowledge-discipline` rather than `transformative-integration`.
Abhinavagupta presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `recognition-or-freedom` rather than `transformation-of-life`.
